title: Async capability adapters — host-injected fetch + KV storage as explicit asynchronous capabilities: wasm_fetch + wasm_kv modules over the existing async-NIF seam, CapabilityError vocabulary identical across the JS/BEAM boundary, refuse-before-suspend INJECTED profile rows with a seal-move commit, host-side cancellation with mandatory process-death auto-abort, edge-worker proof, browser probe authored AND run before close.
purpose:
The async-capability pillar governed by the WPORT-8 section of `docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:160-171`. The arc's SCOPE, verbatim (`:165`): "Implement the selected browser fetch/storage host NIFs as explicit asynchronous capabilities. Completions re-enter through WPORT-4's event-delivery seam and conform to WPORT-5's supported-service profile. This brief does not emulate TCP, a native filesystem, or dirty pools and must not acquire a threaded-runtime dependency." The ACCEPTANCE SHAPE, verbatim (`:169`): "Each selected adapter proves success, host refusal, malformed response, and Promise rejection against a real browser host seam. Completion from true idle schedules exactly one runtime turn and delivers one durable result. Build and dependency checks prove the wasm closure remains free of `threads`, native `net`, native `fs`, and dirty-pool requirements. Tests fail if an adapter blocks, polls, silently maps errors to success, or presents itself as TCP/filesystem emulation."

OQ5 IS DECIDED (arc `:221` open question 5, decided by the domain owner's 2026-07-23 dispositions on the ground pack's eight tear questions — direction ratified as decided text below; the R-numbers earn each at the bytes): the first capability set is FETCH + KV STORAGE. Named workloads: the edge-worker platform class (fetch + KV is the platform's native idiom — with the ground pack's honest gap that the example does not yet exercise them, closed by R8) and the BROWSER-OTP north-star app class. Response streaming and a BEAM-side cancel BIF are BANKED, not built (boundaries below).

GROUND: `docs/design/beamr/briefs/WPORT-8-GROUND-PACK.md` (same directory), evidence pin `fb3efcf`. THIS BRIEF'S PIN: `d57594c` (current main, carrying the WPORT-3 flake-triage landing). Delta `fb3efcf..d57594c` touches `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs` (test module only — the hardened wall + demonstration test), the CI carriers (65→66 exact-count, +1 expected name), probes docs/evidence, and gate-logs — ZERO overlap with any pack citation except pack §3.12's carrier arithmetic, which shifts exactly as its own pin-discipline note predicted (66 names now). Every other pack citation is byte-identical at both commits and citable at `d57594c` unqualified. The build worker MUST re-verify the pin-shift set named in the verification block at its ACTUAL base commit before writing a line — believed state is not citable state.

STRUCTURAL HEADLINE (pack §3.2, kept as a named wall per the domain owner's instruction): the adapters add ZERO new wake-path call sites. `register_async_nif` (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:246-266`) + `HostAsyncNifs::start_callback` (`:1342-1371`) + `start_promise_completion` (`:1373-1408`, rejection twin at `:1386-1393`) + `complete_async` (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:266-275`) + the existing `FailureLeg::Promise` arbiter edge (`lib.rs:1400-1402`) carry the entire async path. The failure-leg closed set stays closed at five (`failure.rs:63`). NO-POLLING is structurally satisfied: request→suspend, completion→one coalesced turn.
task:
DISPOSITIONS (2026-07-23, Artemis Peach, domain owner — decided text; each numbered by the ground pack's §6 TQ numbers):

D1 (TQ1) MFA SURFACE — TWO capability-scoped modules, `wasm_fetch` + `wasm_kv`, ONE injected capability object per module, so least-authority stays legible: a VM with KV but no fetch has a whole module refusing, not half a module. The registration path is shared unchanged for bytecode and native callers (pack §3.1: both consume the same BIF-registry MFA + facility dispatch); any divergence discovered mid-build is a STOP, not a workaround.

D2 (TQ2) SEAL MOVE — its OWN commit (profile rows + seal test + doc tally, nothing else) inside the build branch, message naming the row delta and citing WPORT-8. No pre-approval ceremony: the tear PASS ratifies the seal move (attestation protocol). Silent registration without same-commit rows stays the WPORT-5 registration-must-not-imply-support violation.

D3 (TQ3) ERROR VOCABULARY — ONE new class, `CapabilityError`, the `ArtifactLoadError` mold exactly (named `js_sys::Error`, `"{kind}: {detail}"` message, single `data` property holding a JSON string; mold at `crates/beamr-wasm/src/artifact_loader.rs:532-554`). House error-class census goes to four; a fourth AND fifth is not warranted. The closed kind set is the UNION of the per-adapter slugs, with the adapter named INSIDE the data payload. BEAM-side delivery: `{error, {SlugAtom, DetailBinary}}` — coherent with the native mailbox contract `{ok, Value}`/`{error, Reason}` (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_native.rs:271-274`). CROSS-BOUNDARY VOCABULARY-IDENTITY WALL, mandatory: the JS kind set and the BEAM slug set are the SAME closed set, one closed-set wall each side. `cancelled` IS a slug in the set, delivered through the error twin — honest: the request did not complete.

D4 (TQ4) CANCELLATION — v1 cancellation is HOST-SIDE: the AbortController lives in the capability object, host policy triggers abort; a cancelled request completes with the typed cancellation through the normal seam. PROCESS-DEATH AUTO-ABORT IS MANDATORY: a dying suspended caller aborts its in-flight request, and a completion arriving for a dead pid is a COUNTED no-op. The existing tolerance is cited at the bytes — `complete_async` returns `false` for a pid absent from the process table (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:267-269`) and the completion closure discards that bool (`let _completed =`, `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:1397`) — tolerated today but NOT counted: the counting lands in the beamr-wasm bridge (R6), keeping `crates/beamr` untouched. A BEAM-side cancel BIF is OUT of v1 (banked boundary below). Late cancel = counted no-op per the WPORT-3 stale-token pattern.

D5 (TQ5) ACCEPTANCE SPLIT — Node walls against the in-memory host carry CI (extend the carriers); the browser probe is AUTHORED AND RUN: at least one official sitting recorded as probes-pattern evidence BEFORE WPORT-8 closes (the PROBE-THROTTLE sittings precedent). No permanent CI browser leg now — that is WPORT-9's decision, not preempted here.

D6 (TQ6) WORKLOAD WALL — EXTEND edge-worker: the workload that grounded the selection must be the one that proves it. Closing the ground pack §3.10 honest gap is part of the same act; the README moves same-commit per consumer lockstep (WPORT-2/WPORT-7 precedent).

D7 (TQ7) REFUSAL — profile row category named INJECTED: "works when the named capability object is injected; stable typed refusal otherwise." The refusal is NOT badarg (the args are well-formed): `{error, {capability_missing, CapabilityAtom}}`, and it is SYNCHRONOUS — no suspend, no host turn, no counter motion. REFUSE-BEFORE-SUSPEND is a law of the surface; each profile row names the capability it keys on.

D8 (TQ8) RESPONSE SHAPE — a MAP: `#{status := integer(), headers := map(), body := binary()}` — self-describing and extension-tolerant (streaming later adds keys without breaking shape), matching the codec's object→sorted-map mapping natively (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/convert.rs:81-225`). Request bodies accept binary symmetrically (`Uint8Array` ↔ binary, codec `:227-335`). LIMITS POSTURE: NO new invented limits in v1 — codec depth-256 governs (headers are depth-2, no interaction); an oversized body surfaces as the host's error leg, never a silent trim — no-silent-tradeoffs applies.

LAWS BLOCK (verbatim carriers): NO-POLLING, verbatim from the arc: "NO-POLLING (Tom's ruling): event-loop-driven scheduling — run-to-idle, wake on message; a polling scheduler is a TEAR CONDITION (F-0d acceptance). Any timer whose job is \"check whether something changed\" is a design error." (`docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:21`). ZERO NEW WAKE-PATH CALL SITES: no new `request_external_turn`/`schedule_external_edge` call sites outside tests — the adapters ride the existing Promise-leg completion edge (`lib.rs:1400-1402`); the WPORT-6 R2 closed-site inventory (as re-pinned by WPORT-7) shifts only by line motion, membership unchanged; the build re-verifies the site count at its base. REFUSE-BEFORE-SUSPEND (D7): a capability-missing call returns synchronously with zero arbiter/deadline counter motion. LEAST-AUTHORITY: no ambient global is probed from Rust — the injected capability object is the whole contract (the WPORT-6 injected-fetch law, `artifact_loader.rs:75-79`, generalized). THREADED BYTE-IDENTITY (arc `:115` rank): all adapter code lives in `crates/beamr-wasm`; `crates/beamr` production code is UNTOUCHED by this brief (the D4 counting lands bridge-side by design) — any apparent need to touch shared interpreter/native/scheduler code is a STOP. EARLY-UNDER-CACHED-CLOCK (landed 2026-07-23, `docs/design/beamr/probes/WPORT-3-PROBE-EARLY-FIRE.md` §5): no acceptance test may assert absolute host arm counts or timer promptness across a real fire. NO-KNOB (WPORT-2 Ruling 2): no configuration surface — no retry counts, no timeout knobs, no capability toggles. NO new dependencies (js-sys/wasm-bindgen suffice; AbortController is host-side JS, invisible to Rust). ZERO new `#[allow]`. No `unwrap`/`expect`/`panic` outside `#[cfg(test)]`. Build-environment doctrine per `gates/RUNBOOK.md` at gates `db9630f` (durable team doctrine, supersedes the WPORT-7 brief's older CARGO_TARGET_DIR form): worktrees under `<repo>/.worktrees/`, DEFAULT target dirs (never set CARGO_TARGET_DIR), no builds in temp dirs.

STOP CONDITIONS, binding on the build worker: (1) anywhere this spec is underdetermined at the bytes, STOP and return with alternatives — never improvise a contract; (2) any APPARENT need for a new wake-path call site is a STOP; (3) any APPARENT need to modify `crates/beamr` production code is a STOP (the whole adapter surface is designed to live in `crates/beamr-wasm`); (4) any divergence between bytecode and native callers through the shared registration path (D1) is a STOP; (5) any interaction where refuse-before-suspend cannot be satisfied without counter motion is a STOP; (6) anything touching `crates/beamr/src/timer.rs` is a STOP (cert-resign territory, arc `:92`).

requirements.title: Capability registration surface — two host-injected capability objects, one per module
requirements.spec: Two `WasmVm` registration exports: `register_fetch_capability(&mut self, capability: js_sys::Object)` and `register_kv_capability(&mut self, capability: js_sys::Object)` (exact JS-visible names may follow the existing `register_*` house naming — `lib.rs:141-143`, `:223-225`, `:246-266` — but one export per module, never a generic string-keyed registry). Each stores its object in an adapter bridge (the `HostAsyncNifs`/`HostJsCallbacks` pattern, `lib.rs:1342+`) and registers that module's MFAs against the existing async-NIF trampoline path (`wasm_async_nif_stub`, `lib.rs:1594-1605`) under `Capability::ExternalIo`. Fetch capability object contract: a `request(requestObject, abortSignalSlot)` shape returning a thenable (exact JS method contract fixed at build against the host-adapter walls). KV capability object contract: `get(key)`, `put(key, value)`, `delete(key)`, `list_by_prefix(prefix)` methods, each returning a thenable. Registration is idempotent-last-wins (documented), matching `register_js_callback` semantics. Re-registration after calls are in flight does not disturb in-flight completions.
requirements.acceptance: A VM with only the KV capability registered refuses every `wasm_fetch` MFA with the D7 refusal and answers `wasm_kv` calls; vice versa. Both bytecode callers and native-actor callers reach both adapters through the SAME registered MFAs with no path divergence (D1) — one wall each. No ambient global is reached: the walls run under a host with NO global fetch/KV present, injecting only the capability objects. The wall fails if a generic capability registry, a string-keyed dispatch, or an ambient-global probe appears.

requirements.title: wasm_fetch adapter — request/response over the async-NIF seam
requirements.spec: BEAM surface: `wasm_fetch:request/1` taking a request map (`#{url := binary()}` required; optional `method` (binary, default <<"GET">>), `headers` (map of binary→binary), `body` (binary)). The adapter marshals the request map to a JS object via the existing codec (`convert.rs:68-79`), invokes the fetch capability's request method, suspends the caller through the existing seam (`context.request_suspend(None)`, `lib.rs:1360-1367` pattern), and delivers the completion through `complete_async` + the Promise-leg arbiter edge — ZERO new wake-path call sites. Success: `{ok, #{status := integer(), headers := map(), body := binary()}}` (D8; non-UTF-8 bodies ride the codec's Uint8Array↔binary mapping, `convert.rs:227-335`). Failure legs, all through the error twin as `{error, {SlugAtom, DetailBinary}}` (D3): host refusal (capability method throws synchronously or returns a non-thenable), malformed response (thenable resolves to a value that does not satisfy the response contract), rejection (thenable rejects), cancellation (`cancelled` slug — the abort path, R6). No streaming: the response body is one binary, delivered whole.
requirements.acceptance: Walls prove, against the in-memory/scripted host: success round-trip with status/headers/body intact including a non-UTF-8 body; host refusal, malformed response, and rejection each delivering the exact `{error, {Slug, Detail}}` shape; completion from TRUE IDLE schedules exactly one arbiter turn (arbiter counter arithmetic, respecting EARLY-UNDER-CACHED-CLOCK — no absolute-count assertions across real timer fires) and delivers one durable result; a native-actor caller receives the identical contract via its mailbox (`{ok, V}`/`{error, R}`, `wasm_native.rs:271-274`). The wall fails if an error leg silently maps to success, if the adapter blocks or polls, or if the response shape is a tuple/list rather than the D8 map.

requirements.title: wasm_kv adapter — get/put/delete/list_by_prefix
requirements.spec: BEAM surface: `wasm_kv:get/1` (Key binary → `{ok, Value binary}` | `{ok, undefined}` for absent — exact absent-shape fixed at build against all three host shapes, atom `undefined` preferred as the codec-native null), `wasm_kv:put/2` (Key, Value binaries → `{ok, true}`), `wasm_kv:delete/1` (Key → `{ok, true}`, idempotent), `wasm_kv:list_by_prefix/1` (Prefix binary → `{ok, [Key binary]}` in LEXICOGRAPHIC key order — the one ordering satisfiable by Workers KV, an IndexedDB adapter, and an in-memory host without drift). Same seam, same suspension, same error twin and slug vocabulary as R2 (kv failures use the shared closed set with the adapter named in the JS data payload). Keys and values are binaries end to end; no term serialization is invented (a caller wanting terms uses `term_to_binary` when B-086 lands — out of scope).
requirements.acceptance: Walls prove against the in-memory host: put→get round-trip byte-exact; get-absent shape; delete idempotence; list_by_prefix lexicographic order and prefix correctness (empty prefix = full listing); each failure leg typed per D3; suspension/one-turn delivery identical to R2's counters wall. The wall fails on any invented value encoding, any non-lexicographic listing, or any silent success on a host error.

requirements.title: CapabilityError — fourth house error class, one vocabulary on both sides of the boundary
requirements.spec: One new JS error class `CapabilityError`, the `ArtifactLoadError` mold exactly (`artifact_loader.rs:532-554`): named `js_sys::Error`, message `"{kind}: {detail}"`, ONE `data` property holding a JSON string `{"adapter":"fetch"|"kv","kind":<slug>,"detail":<string>}`. The closed kind set is ONE union set (D3), declared as a Rust const `CAPABILITY_KIND_SLUGS` beside the class: `capability_missing`, `refused`, `malformed_response`, `rejected`, `cancelled` (exact membership may gain kv-specific slugs at build ONLY via the tear — the set is closed, additions are tear-visible). The adapter bridge normalizes EVERY host-side failure into this vocabulary before delivery; BEAM-side delivery converts kind slug → atom in `{error, {SlugAtom, DetailBinary}}`. The JS class surfaces wherever adapter failures reach JS observers (at minimum: the walls construct instances through the same minter the bridge uses).
requirements.acceptance: Cross-boundary vocabulary-identity walls, one per side (D3, mandatory): a Rust wall asserting `CAPABILITY_KIND_SLUGS` is closed and exact (the `failure_tests.rs:274-295` pattern), and a wall asserting the BEAM-delivered slug atoms are EXACTLY that set (constructing each error leg and collecting the atoms). The walls additionally PIN the one deliberate detail-slot asymmetry as law (tear amendment A2, 2026-07-23): `capability_missing` carries the CapabilityAtom (D7's shape) while the operational slugs (`refused`, `malformed_response`, `rejected`, `cancelled`) carry DetailBinary (D3's shape) — slug-set identity across the boundary AND both detail shapes asserted explicitly, so the asymmetry reads as designed, never as drift. The minter wall proves name/message/data mechanics byte-parallel to `ArtifactLoadError`. The wall fails if any error reaches BEAM code or JS code with a kind outside the closed set, or if the two sets diverge.

requirements.title: Refuse-before-suspend — synchronous capability_missing refusal with zero counter motion
requirements.spec: A `wasm_fetch`/`wasm_kv` MFA invoked with its module's capability object NOT registered returns `{error, {capability_missing, CapabilityAtom}}` SYNCHRONOUSLY (D7): no `request_suspend`, no host call, no arbiter turn, no deadline-service interaction, no counter motion anywhere. `CapabilityAtom` names the module's capability (`fetch` | `kv`). The refusal is NOT badarg — well-formed args against an uninjected capability are a stable typed refusal, distinct from arg-shape errors (which stay badarg-class per the WPORT-5 vocabulary). REFUSE-BEFORE-SUSPEND is a law of the surface: the capability check precedes every other effect.
requirements.acceptance: Walls prove: refusal value shape for both modules; the caller is NOT suspended (it continues in the same slice); arbiter and deadline counter snapshots before/after the refusing call are IDENTICAL (the WPORT-3 snapshot machinery); a native-actor caller receives the same refusal shape. Malformed args on a REGISTERED capability still badarg (one wall pinning the distinction). The wall fails on any suspend, any host turn, or any counter delta during a refusal.

requirements.title: Cancellation and process-death auto-abort — host-side abort, counted dead-pid no-ops
requirements.spec: Host-side cancellation (D4): the capability object owns its AbortController machinery; when the host aborts an in-flight request, the thenable settles and the bridge delivers `{error, {cancelled, Detail}}` through the NORMAL completion seam — never a hang, never a poll. PROCESS-DEATH AUTO-ABORT, mandatory — trigger seam NAMED (tear amendment A1, 2026-07-23): the arbiter's exit-observation sweep — the same observation that settles ExitWaiters (`await_exit`'s per-pid waiters machinery, `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:809-855` at `d57594c`) — is the auto-abort trigger point. When the arbiter observes a pid's exit, it fires the abort hooks for that pid's in-flight capability requests (host-direction call — zero wake-path involvement, zero `crates/beamr` motion). The bridge's in-flight tracking is wired into the SAME per-pid structure family as the exit waiters, so there is ONE death-observation point, never two. The abort-signal slot passed to the fetch capability at request time (R1) is the mechanism: the bridge holds the paired trigger. DEAD-PID COMPLETION IS A COUNTED NO-OP: the existing tolerance (`complete_async` returns `false` for an absent pid, `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:267-269`; the completion closure discards the bool at `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:1397`) gains a bridge-side counter (`dead_pid_completions` in the adapter bridge's counter group, COUNTED and falsifiable per the WPORT-3 counter law) — `crates/beamr` stays untouched. Late cancel (abort after completion already delivered) is likewise a counted no-op per the WPORT-3 stale-token pattern.
requirements.acceptance: Walls prove: a host-aborted in-flight request delivers exactly one `{error, {cancelled, _}}` completion; a caller killed mid-flight triggers the abort hook (scripted host observes the abort) and the subsequent completion increments `dead_pid_completions` by exactly one with no wake, no turn, no delivery; an abort arriving after successful completion is a counted no-op with the result undisturbed. The wall fails if a cancelled request hangs, if a dying caller leaks an un-aborted in-flight request, or if a dead-pid completion moves any counter other than its own.

requirements.title: Profile seal move — INJECTED rows, own commit
requirements.spec: Every new BEAM-facing MFA (`wasm_fetch:request/1`, `wasm_kv:get/1`, `wasm_kv:put/2`, `wasm_kv:delete/1`, `wasm_kv:list_by_prefix/1` — plus any build-discovered companions, tear-visible) lands in `docs/design/beamr/BROWSER-BIF-PROFILE.md` under the new row category INJECTED (D7): "works when the named capability object is injected; stable typed refusal otherwise", each row naming the capability it keys on. The seal move is its OWN commit (D2): profile rows + `crates/beamr-wasm/tests/profile_seal.rs` update + doc tally (197 → new total), NOTHING else in the commit, message naming the row delta and citing WPORT-8. The tear PASS ratifies the move (attestation protocol).
requirements.acceptance: The seal test passes with the new exact key set; the doc tallies match the row count; `git show` of the seal commit touches exactly the profile doc + seal test. The wall fails if any registered MFA lacks a same-commit row (the WPORT-5 violation), if the seal commit carries unrelated changes, or if the INJECTED category text drifts from D7's definition.

requirements.title: Edge-worker proof — the grounding workload exercises both adapters
requirements.spec: EXTEND `examples/edge-worker` (D6, never a new example): the worker registers a fetch capability (backed by the platform's fetch binding) and a KV capability (backed by an in-memory/Miniflare KV shim in tests), and at least one request path exercises `wasm_fetch:request/1` and the `wasm_kv` surface from BEAM code end to end. This closes the ground pack §3.10 honest gap: the workload that grounded the selection proves it. The README's capability section moves in the SAME COMMIT (consumer lockstep, the WPORT-7 recovery-note precedent at `README.md:61`).
requirements.acceptance: The edge-worker test suite (Miniflare) proves a request whose BEAM handler performs a fetch and a KV round-trip, with the response assembled from both; a capability-missing configuration serves the typed refusal through to an observable HTTP response (no hang). The wall fails if the example bypasses the BEAM adapters (host-side fetch/KV with results injected as plain args), or if the README lags the surface.

requirements.title: Browser probe — authored AND run before close; Node walls carry CI
requirements.spec: Author `docs/design/beamr/probes/WPORT-8-PROBE-CAPABILITY.md` in the house probes pattern AND record at least one OFFICIAL sitting before WPORT-8 closes (D5 — the PROBE-THROTTLE sittings precedent): a real browser context exercising the real generated bundle with a real fetch capability (live endpoint or service-worker-served), proving success, rejection, cancellation-under-abort, and one-turn-per-completion against real browser Promise semantics; evidence committed under `probes/evidence/` with environment recorded (the EARLY-FIRE evidence conventions, including truthfully-named emitter fields). Node walls against the in-memory host carry CI: the new test names extend the pinned carriers and the exact-count line moves same-commit (66 → new total; all count carriers moved per commit per the WPORT-7 discipline). NO permanent CI browser leg (WPORT-9's decision).
requirements.acceptance: The probe doc exists with the sitting's observations attached and environment recorded; CI carriers enumerate every new wall by exact name with the exact-count summary line moved in the same commit that adds each test. The wall fails if the probe stays AUTHORED-NOT-RUN at close, if any committed evidence field name misdescribes its value, or if a carrier count moves without its names (or names without count).

boundaries: BANKED — response streaming (D8/OQ5): no streaming keys, no chunk delivery, no ReadableStream mapping in v1. The D8 map shape is extension-tolerant precisely so streaming can later ADD keys; opening it requires a consumer requirement and the reviewer of record's word (the WPORT-10 banking pattern, arc `:213`).

BANKED — BEAM-side cancel BIF (D4): the WPORT-4 seam suspends the caller, so a cancel BIF implies a second-process ref-holder model no named workload requires. Opens on a consumer requirement plus the domain owner's word. v1 cancellation is host-side only.
NO TCP or filesystem presentation (arc `:165`): the adapters are fetch and KV, named as such; no inet/gen_tcp/file MFA aliases, no path semantics on KV keys.

DEPENDENCY CLOSURE UNTOUCHED: `beamr-wasm` keeps `beamr` at `default-features = false, features = ["cooperative","json"]` (`crates/beamr-wasm/Cargo.toml:18-21`); no threads/net/fs/jit/embedded feature appears anywhere in the closure; CI's two wasm32 check legs and the seal test remain the wall. NO new crate dependencies.

ZERO new wake-path call sites (`request_external_turn`/`schedule_external_edge` outside tests) — completions ride the existing Promise leg; the closed-site inventory membership is unchanged; any apparent need is a STOP.

ZERO `crates/beamr` production changes — the entire adapter surface lives in `crates/beamr-wasm` (D4's counting is bridge-side by design); threaded byte-identity holds trivially; any apparent need is a STOP.
NO invented limits (D8): codec depth-256 governs; no size caps, no header-count caps; an oversized body is the host's error leg, never a silent trim — no-silent-tradeoffs.
NO-KNOB: no configuration surface — no retry counts, timeout knobs, or capability toggles; the host owns policy (retries, timeouts, abort) inside its capability object.
SEAL DISCIPLINE (D2): no MFA registration without same-commit INJECTED profile rows; the seal move is its own commit.
EARLY-UNDER-CACHED-CLOCK (landed 2026-07-23): no wall asserts absolute host arm counts or timer promptness across a real fire; counter walls use the identity/gauge forms.

Build-environment doctrine per `gates/RUNBOOK.md` at gates `db9630f` (durable team doctrine, supersedes the WPORT-7 brief's older CARGO_TARGET_DIR form): worktrees under `<repo>/.worktrees/`, DEFAULT target dirs (never set CARGO_TARGET_DIR), no builds in temp dirs.
verification:
PIN RE-VERIFY TABLE, binding at the build worker's ACTUAL base commit before writing a line: (1) the async-NIF seam lines — `register_async_nif` `lib.rs:246-266`, `wasm_async_nif_stub` `:1594-1605`, `HostAsyncNifs::start_callback` `:1342-1371`, `start_promise_completion` `:1373-1408` with the rejection twin `:1386-1393` and the Promise-leg edge `:1400-1402` (these shift with the WPORT-3 landing's test-module motion — re-pin exact lines); (2) `complete_async` absent-pid return `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:266-275` and the discarded bool `lib.rs:1397`; (3) native delivery `wasm_native.rs:271-274`; (4) `LEG_SLUGS` closed five `failure.rs:63`; (5) CI carriers — 66 names + exact-count line at the base (the WPORT-3 landing moved 65→66); (6) profile tallies 197 at `:68`/`:78` and the absence of fetch/storage rows; (7) codec entry points `convert.rs:25-79`, `:227-335`; (8) the injected-fetch law `artifact_loader.rs:75-79` and the `ArtifactLoadError` mold `:532-554`; (9) `NativeServices` five-service injection `wasm.rs:792-811`.

GATE BAR at the final head: the four-leg battery (fmt / clippy -D warnings / wasm32-check / tests) via the gates runner, COLD-CACHE standard (the 2026-07-23 verification precedent: clippy and wasm32-check logs must show the closure crates actually compiled — a warm no-op log is a hold); the full wasm-bindgen suite green with the new exact count; every new wall name in the carriers; the borrow wall untouched. EVIDENCE COMMITS: gates evidence committed in `gate-logs/` at the final tree; probe sitting evidence under `probes/evidence/` with environment and truthfully-named fields.

COMMIT DISCIPLINE: red-first where behavior is pinned (each failing wall committed before the code that greens it, per the seat's standing rule); the seal move its own commit (D2); count carriers move with their names per commit (WPORT-7 discipline); consumer-lockstep commits pair surface + README. Push early, keep pushing, stage explicit paths only. Route ready-for-tear through Anubis Le Snak; tear at Artemis Peach's seat; verdicts to Waffles the Terrible.
